refined_fr.refined_fr: Ensure the NGHE solution achieves or exceeds the customer-defined operational availability target by maintaining system uptime, minimizing downtime, and enabling rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability is a composite metric dependent on system reliability, maintainability, and operational readiness. Decomposing the availability requirement into sub-functions addressing uptime, downtime minimization, and fault recovery enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure operational availability over the evaluation period using telemetry data and maintenance logs; verify uptime percentage meets or exceeds target.
sub_frs: Minimize system downtime due to faults or maintenance
Enable rapid recovery from system faults

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms
Predictive maintenance and fault detection algorithms
Automated fault recovery and restart procedures
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms

potential_sources: Dependency between fault detection and recovery mechanisms
Interaction between redundant components and system control
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates availability improvement via redundancy, minimizing coupling with other DPs focused on maintenance and recovery.

assumptions_risks: Redundancy will not introduce excessive complexity or new failure modes
Failover mechanisms are reliable and tested under operational conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating predictive maintenance DP in next decomposition level to address downtime minimization.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for operational uptime and failover hardware/software

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time system status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution and Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication for failover coordination and remote monitoring

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of redundancy hardware/software; low diffusion as failover logic is localized.
Contains system-level availability monitoring but less direct control over redundancy.
Limited containment; mainly consumer of availability data.
Monitors safety alerts related to availability but does not implement redundancy.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Redundancy and failover mechanisms are best contained within the Vehicle module to localize complexity and enable rapid fault isolation.
containment_risks: Potential for increased hardware complexity impacting maintainability
Risk of incomplete integration with system-level monitoring in NGHE Solution
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Enhance interface definitions between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for availability status reporting.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundancy and failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions related to uptime and fault tolerance with clear logical mapping.

diffusion_risks: Some availability monitoring logic may be duplicated between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module effectively contains redundancy mechanisms; interfaces to NGHE Solution are well-defined for status reporting.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability can be verified via telemetry data collected through defined interfaces; module ownership is clear.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The Vehicle module ownership of redundancy aligns well with physical system boundaries and operational responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize interface protocols for availability telemetry between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Plan next-level decomposition to address downtime minimization and fault recovery in other modules.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to fully cover all sub-FRs related to availability, especially downtime minimization and fault recovery.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize system downtime due to faults or maintenance
Enable rapid recovery from system faults

revise.why: To decompose availability into comprehensive sub-FRs that enable targeted DP and IM assignments for all aspects of availability.

assumptions: Redundancy mechanisms will be effective in improving availability.
Interfaces between modules will support necessary data exchange.
open_risks: Potential complexity and maintainability issues from redundancy hardware/software.
Incomplete coverage of availability aspects if other sub-FRs are not addressed.
evidence_needed: Verification plans for telemetry and failover mechanisms.
